title: Buttery Silky Canadian Challots Poutine
description: A rich, comforting weeknight Canadian dish featuring roasted challots and a creamy bhuna sauce, perfect for a cozy dinner. This recipe makes a generous serving of 4-6 people.
instructions: Peel the challots and cut them into 1-inch cubes.
On a baking sheet, toss the challot cubes with 2 tablespoons of butter, salt, and pepper until they are evenly coated.
Roast the challots in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until tender and golden brown.
In a large saucepan, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat.
Add the flour and whisk to form a roux, cooking for 1-2 minutes.
Slowly pour in the milk, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes, until thickened.
Stir in the bhuna sauce and nutmeg.
Once the challots are done roasting, toss them in the saucepan with the bhuna sauce mixture until well coated.
Transfer the challot mixture to a serving dish and top with shredded gruyère cheese.
Place the dish under the broiler and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
